articles.headline: PM Unveils 'Bharat Logistics & Infrastructure Mission 2047' to Boost Connectivity and Economy
articles.explainer:
• Prime Minister launches ambitious 'Bharat Logistics & Infrastructure Mission 2047' aiming for world-class connectivity by India's centenary of independence.
• The mission integrates existing initiatives like PM Gati Shakti with new investments in multi-modal logistics parks, high-speed rail corridors, and port modernization.
• Focus on reducing logistics costs from 14% to 8% of GDP, enhancing global competitiveness and creating millions of jobs.
• Utilizes advanced technologies like AI, IoT, and blockchain for efficient supply chain management and project monitoring.
• Public-private partnership model to be adopted extensively, inviting significant private sector investment and technological expertise.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
prelimsPoints: Mission name: Bharat Logistics & Infrastructure Mission 2047.
Target year: 2047 (India's 100th year of independence).
Key objective: Reduce logistics costs to 8% of GDP.
Integration with: PM Gati Shakti National Master Plan.
Technologies used: AI, IoT, blockchain.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of integrated infrastructure development for India's economic growth and global competitiveness.
Analyze how the mission leverages technology and PPP models to overcome traditional challenges in infrastructure projects.
Examine the potential socio-economic impacts, including job creation, regional development, and reduction in logistics costs.
Critically evaluate the challenges in implementation, such as land acquisition, environmental clearances, and inter-ministerial coordination.
articles.imageDescription: Prime Minister addressing a gathering at the launch of Bharat Logistics & Infrastructure Mission 2047, with a digital rendering of modern infrastructure in the background.
articles.trivia: India's logistics sector is one of the largest employers, and its efficiency is crucial for the 'Make in India' initiative.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Clarifies 'Right to be Forgotten' within Digital India Context
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court, in a landmark judgment, provided a detailed framework for the 'Right to be Forgotten' in India.
• The ruling emphasizes a balance between an individual's right to privacy and the public's right to information, especially for historical or public interest records.
• It clarifies that the right is not absolute and will be subject to conditions like the nature of information, its public relevance, and the impact on free speech.
• The court directed the government to consider legislative steps to incorporate this right explicitly within India's data protection framework.
• This judgment is expected to have significant implications for online content publishers, data aggregators, and social media platforms.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: The judgment clarifies the scope of 'Right to be Forgotten' in India.
It balances individual privacy (Article 21) with public right to information.
The right is not absolute and has conditions.
Court urged government to legislate on the matter.
mainsPoints: Discuss the constitutional basis and jurisprudential evolution of the 'Right to be Forgotten' in India.
Analyze the challenges in balancing individual privacy rights with freedom of speech and public access to information in the digital age.
Examine the potential impact of this judgment on media, social media platforms, and the upcoming data protection legislation.
Suggest legislative and technological measures required for effective implementation of the 'Right to be Forgotten'.
articles.imageDescription: A gavel resting on a legal book, symbolizing a Supreme Court judgment, with a blurred background of digital data.
articles.trivia: The 'Right to be Forgotten' originated in European Union's General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR).

articles.headline: India-France Joint Naval Exercise 'Varuna-25' Concludes, Enhancing Maritime Security Cooperation
articles.explainer:
• The 23rd edition of the bilateral naval exercise 'Varuna' between India and France successfully concluded in the Arabian Sea.
• The exercise focused on advanced anti-submarine warfare, anti-air warfare, and surface combat operations, enhancing interoperability.
• Participation included frontline destroyers, frigates, submarines, and maritime patrol aircraft from both navies.
• 'Varuna-25' underscored the strategic partnership between India and France in ensuring a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific.
• The exercise also included professional exchanges and cross-deck landings, strengthening mutual understanding and best practices.

Evaluate the challenges and opportunities in expanding defence ties with European partners like France.
articles.imageDescription: Indian and French naval ships sailing in formation during the Varuna-25 exercise.
articles.trivia: The 'Varuna' exercise began in 1993 and was christened 'Varuna' in 2001.

articles.headline: National Green Hydrogen Mission Achieves Key Milestone, First Commercial Plant Operational
articles.explainer:
• India's first large-scale commercial green hydrogen production plant, under the National Green Hydrogen Mission, has commenced operations in Gujarat.
• The plant utilizes renewable energy sources for electrolysis, producing green hydrogen with zero carbon emissions.
• This milestone is crucial for India's target of producing 5 million tonnes of green hydrogen annually by 2030.
• The project aims to decarbonize hard-to-abate sectors like refineries, fertilizers, and steel, while also fostering export opportunities.
• Government incentives and policy support have been instrumental in attracting private investment for such large-scale green energy projects.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Infrastructure: Energy.

Examine the role of government policies and incentives, such as the National Green Hydrogen Mission, in fostering this nascent industry.
Evaluate the potential of green hydrogen to transform India's industrial landscape and create new export opportunities.
articles.imageDescription: An industrial facility with solar panels in the background, symbolizing a green hydrogen production plant.
articles.trivia: Hydrogen is the most abundant chemical substance in the universe, but 'green' hydrogen refers specifically to that produced using renewable energy.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Launches 'NISAR-2' Satellite for Advanced Earth Observation
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully launched 'NISAR-2', an advanced Earth observation satellite, from Satish Dhawan Space Centre.
• 'NISAR-2' is a follow-up to the joint ISRO-NASA NISAR mission, focusing on high-resolution imaging for climate studies and disaster management.
• The satellite is equipped with advanced Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) technology, enabling all-weather, day-night observations of Earth's surface.
• Data from NISAR-2 will be crucial for monitoring changes in ecosystems, ice-sheet collapses, natural hazards like earthquakes and tsunamis, and agricultural mapping.
• This mission reinforces India's capabilities in space technology and its commitment to global environmental monitoring.

Analyze how SAR technology contributes to disaster prediction, mitigation, and climate change studies.

Evaluate the socio-economic benefits derived from advanced remote sensing data for various sectors.
articles.imageDescription: A rocket launching into space, with 'ISRO' logo visible, symbolizing the NISAR-2 mission.
articles.trivia: NISAR stands for NASA-ISRO Synthetic Aperture Radar, a joint mission between the two space agencies.

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate at 6.5%, Signals Vigilance on Inflationary Pressures
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) unanimously decided to keep the repo rate unchanged at 6.5%.
• This decision reflects the MPC's cautious approach amidst fluctuating global commodity prices and domestic food inflation concerns.
• RBI reiterated its commitment to aligning inflation with the target while supporting growth, maintaining its 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance.
• Experts suggest the central bank is closely monitoring monsoon progress and its impact on food prices, as well as global economic uncertainties.
• Future policy actions will be data-dependent, with a focus on ensuring financial stability and sustainable economic recovery.

Examine the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing inflation control with the imperative for economic recovery amidst global uncertainties.
Evaluate the effectiveness of the 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance in the current economic scenario.
articles.imageDescription: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) logo, with a graph showing stable interest rates in the background.
articles.trivia: The MPC has six members: three from RBI (including the Governor) and three external members appointed by the government.

articles.headline: Nation Remembers Tribal Freedom Fighter Birsa Munda on His Death Anniversary
articles.explainer:
• Tributes were paid across the nation to legendary tribal freedom fighter Birsa Munda on his 125th death anniversary (June 9, 1900).
• Birsa Munda led the 'Ulgulan' (Great Tumult) movement against British colonial rule and the exploitation by local landlords and moneylenders in the late 19th century.
• He is revered as 'Dharti Abba' (Father of the Earth) by tribal communities, especially in Jharkhand, for his efforts to protect their land, culture, and rights.
• His movement sought to establish 'Munda Raj' and challenged the British legal system that alienated tribals from their traditional land and forest rights.
• The government highlighted his contributions to India's freedom struggle and reiterated commitment to tribal welfare and preservation of their heritage.
gsSubTopics: The Freedom Struggle — its various stages and important contributors/contributions from different parts of the country.

Movement led: 'Ulgulan' (Great Tumult).
Region of activity: Chota Nagpur Plateau (present-day Jharkhand).
Fought against: British colonial rule, Dikus (outsiders).
mainsPoints: Discuss the nature and significance of tribal movements, such as the 'Ulgulan' led by Birsa Munda, in the context of India's freedom struggle.
Analyze how Birsa Munda's leadership addressed socio-economic and cultural exploitation faced by tribal communities under British rule.

articles.imageDescription: A portrait of Birsa Munda, a revered tribal leader, with traditional tribal motifs in the background.
articles.trivia: Birsa Munda is the only tribal leader whose portrait hangs in the Central Hall of the Indian Parliament.

articles.headline: Parliamentary Panel Recommends Reforms in Anti-Defection Law to Ensure Stability and Democracy
articles.explainer:
• A Parliamentary Standing Committee on Law and Justice submitted a report recommending significant amendments to the Anti-Defection Law (Tenth Schedule).
• Key recommendations include restricting the Speaker's discretionary powers in defection cases and setting a strict time limit for decisions.
• The panel suggested that defection should only apply to votes on confidence motions or budget, not all legislative votes, to encourage intra-party dissent.
• It also proposed that independent committees or the Election Commission should adjudicate defection cases, rather than the Speaker.
• The proposed reforms aim to prevent political instability caused by frequent defections and uphold the spirit of parliamentary democracy.
gsSubTopics: Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these.
Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act.
prelimsPoints: Recommendations by: Parliamentary Standing Committee on Law and Justice.
Focus: Reforms in Anti-Defection Law (Tenth Schedule).
Key proposal: Restrict Speaker's powers, set time limits.
Other proposal: Defection only for confidence/budget votes.
Alternative adjudicating body suggested: Election Commission or independent committee.
mainsPoints: Discuss the genesis and objectives of the Anti-Defection Law in India and its impact on parliamentary democracy.
Analyze the criticisms leveled against the current provisions of the Tenth Schedule, particularly concerning the Speaker's role and the scope of defection.
Evaluate the proposed reforms by the Parliamentary panel and their potential to enhance political stability and intra-party democracy.
Examine the constitutional and legal implications of shifting the adjudication of defection cases from the Speaker to other bodies.
articles.imageDescription: The Parliament of India building, symbolizing the legislative process and proposed reforms.
articles.trivia: The Anti-Defection Law was added to the Constitution as the Tenth Schedule in 1985 by the 52nd Amendment Act.

articles.headline: New Cyber Security Framework for Critical Infrastructure Rolled Out by CERT-In
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Computer Emergency Response Team (CERT-In) has released an updated and comprehensive cybersecurity framework for critical information infrastructure (CII).
• The framework mandates stringent security audits, real-time threat monitoring, and incident response protocols for sectors like power, finance, telecom, and transportation.
• It emphasizes a 'zero-trust' architecture and greater collaboration between government agencies and private sector operators of CII.
• The move comes amidst a rising number of sophisticated cyberattacks globally, posing significant risks to national security and economic stability.
• Training and capacity building for cybersecurity professionals within CII entities are also a key component of the new guidelines.
gsSubTopics: Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges, basics of cyber security; money-laundering and its prevention.
Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.

Target: Critical Information Infrastructure (CII).

mainsPoints: Discuss the growing threat of cyberattacks on critical infrastructure and its implications for national security and economic stability.
Analyze the key features of the new cybersecurity framework by CERT-In and how it aims to enhance the resilience of CII.
Examine the challenges in implementing a robust cybersecurity architecture across diverse critical sectors in India.
Suggest measures for strengthening public-private partnerships and international cooperation in combating cyber threats.
articles.imageDescription: A digital padlock icon superimposed on a network of interconnected nodes, representing cybersecurity for critical infrastructure.
articles.trivia: CERT-In is the national agency for performing functions in the area of cyber security as per IT (Amendment) Act 2008.

articles.headline: India Pledges Support for Global Plastics Treaty at UNEP Negotiations
articles.explainer:
• India reaffirmed its commitment to a legally binding global plastics treaty at the ongoing United Nations Environment Programme (UNEP) negotiations.
• The Indian delegation emphasized a comprehensive approach focusing on the entire lifecycle of plastics, from production to disposal.
• Key proposals from India include promoting circular economy principles, enhancing recycling infrastructure, and supporting developing nations with technology transfer and finance.
• India highlighted its domestic efforts, such as the ban on single-use plastics and extended producer responsibility (EPR) guidelines.
• The treaty aims to address plastic pollution, which poses a severe threat to marine ecosystems, human health, and climate.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.

Evaluate the effectiveness of domestic policies like the single-use plastic ban and EPR in addressing plastic pollution.
articles.imageDescription: A globe surrounded by plastic waste, with a hand reaching out to clean it, symbolizing global efforts against plastic pollution.
articles.trivia: Estimates suggest that by 2050, there could be more plastic than fish in the ocean by weight.

articles.headline: Government Launches 'Skill India 2.0' with Focus on AI, Green Jobs, and Future Skills
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship launched 'Skill India 2.0', an enhanced version of its flagship skilling program.
• The new initiative prioritizes training in emerging technologies like Artificial Intelligence (AI), Machine Learning (ML), Data Science, and Green Skills.
• It aims to align India's workforce with the demands of the fourth industrial revolution and the transition to a green economy.
• The program incorporates industry partnerships, digital learning platforms, and accreditation for prior learning to ensure high-quality, relevant training.
• 'Skill India 2.0' targets empowering youth, women, and marginalized communities for better employment opportunities and entrepreneurship.

articles.headline: Centre-State Dialogue on Fiscal Decentralization Gains Momentum, GST Council Meets
articles.explainer:
• A high-level dialogue between the Centre and States on enhancing fiscal decentralization and rationalizing tax structures is showing progress.
• The Goods and Services Tax (GST) Council, in its latest meeting, discussed proposals for revenue sharing mechanisms and streamlining compliance.
• States have advocated for greater fiscal autonomy and predictability in transfers from the central government, especially post-GST implementation.
• Discussions include the role of the Finance Commission in ensuring equitable distribution of resources and addressing state-specific fiscal challenges.
• The ongoing dialogue aims to strengthen cooperative federalism and foster a more robust and equitable fiscal architecture in the country.
gsSubTopics: Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.

articles.imageDescription: A map of India with states highlighted, symbolizing Centre-State financial relations and federalism.
articles.trivia: The GST Council is a constitutional body (Article 279A) for making recommendations to the Union and State Governments on issues related to Goods and Services Tax.

articles.headline: Indigenous mRNA Vaccine for Dengue Shows Promising Results in Phase-III Trials
articles.explainer:
• India's first indigenous mRNA vaccine candidate for Dengue fever has demonstrated high efficacy and safety in its Phase-III clinical trials.
• Developed by a Pune-based biotechnology firm, the vaccine targets all four serotypes of the Dengue virus, offering broad protection.
• The success marks a significant leap in India's vaccine development capabilities, leveraging cutting-edge mRNA technology, previously used for COVID-19 vaccines.
• Regulatory approval is anticipated soon, paving the way for mass production and deployment to combat the perennial public health challenge of Dengue.
• This development is crucial for reducing the burden on healthcare systems and improving public health outcomes in Dengue-endemic regions.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health.

mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of indigenous vaccine development, particularly mRNA technology, for India's public health security and self-reliance.
Analyze the challenges posed by Dengue fever in India and how a new vaccine can impact disease burden and healthcare systems.
Examine the regulatory processes involved in vaccine approval and the ethical considerations in clinical trials.
Evaluate India's potential to become a global hub for vaccine research, development, and manufacturing.
articles.imageDescription: A scientist in a lab coat holding a vial, with a microscope and other lab equipment in the background, symbolizing vaccine research.
articles.trivia: Dengue fever is caused by a virus transmitted by Aedes aegypti mosquitoes, also known as the 'tiger mosquito'.

articles.headline: NEP 2020 Implementation: Focus on Foundational Literacy and Numeracy in Rural Areas
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education released a progress report on the implementation of National Education Policy (NEP) 2020, highlighting efforts in foundational literacy and numeracy (FLN).
• A special emphasis is being placed on rural and underserved areas to ensure equitable access to quality early childhood education.
• Initiatives include extensive teacher training programs, development of engaging learning materials in regional languages, and community participation.
• The 'NIPUN Bharat Mission' continues to be the cornerstone for achieving universal FLN by 2026-27.
• The report underlines the importance of integrating technology and play-based learning methods to make education more accessible and effective for young learners.

articles.imageDescription: Children in a rural classroom happily learning with interactive materials, symbolizing foundational education efforts.
articles.trivia: NEP 2020 replaced the 34-year-old National Policy on Education (1986).

articles.headline: India Deepens Energy Cooperation with Middle Eastern Nations Amid Global Volatility
articles.explainer:
• India has intensified its energy diplomacy with key Middle Eastern countries to ensure stable and diversified energy supplies.
• Bilateral agreements focused on long-term oil and gas contracts, strategic petroleum reserves, and investments in renewable energy projects.
• The move is aimed at mitigating risks from global energy price volatility and geopolitical uncertainties.
• India emphasized its role as a reliable energy consumer and a growing market for green energy technologies.
• Discussions also included collaboration on petrochemicals, hydrogen, and carbon capture technologies, moving beyond traditional fossil fuels.

articles.headline: Monsoon Arrives Early in Kerala, IMD Predicts Above-Normal Rains This Season
articles.explainer:
• The Southwest Monsoon has made an early onset over Kerala, signaling the beginning of the rainy season in India.
• The India Meteorological Department (IMD) announced that conditions are favorable for its further advancement across the peninsula.
• IMD's long-range forecast predicts an 'above-normal' monsoon for the current year, which is crucial for India's agriculture-dependent economy.
• This forecast brings relief for farmers and is expected to positively impact agricultural output and rural demand.
• However, concerns remain regarding the distribution of rainfall and potential for extreme weather events in certain regions.

articles.headline: High Court Directs States to Expedite Compensation for Victims of Mob Lynching
articles.explainer:
• A prominent High Court issued directives to state governments to formulate and implement victim compensation schemes specifically for mob lynching incidents.
• The court expressed serious concern over the delay in providing financial aid and rehabilitation to victims and their families.
• It emphasized that compensation should be prompt, adequate, and not contingent upon the conviction of the accused, as per Supreme Court guidelines.
• The judgment highlighted the state's responsibility to protect its citizens and ensure social justice, especially for vulnerable sections.
• This ruling is expected to push states to adopt more proactive measures to address mob violence and support its victims.

articles.headline: India's Exports Surge in May, Driven by Engineering Goods and Pharmaceuticals
articles.explainer:
• India's merchandise exports witnessed a significant surge in May 2025, showing robust growth across several key sectors.
• Engineering goods, pharmaceuticals, and electronic items emerged as the primary drivers of this export growth, reflecting global demand.
• The Ministry of Commerce and Industry attributed the positive trend to diversified export markets and government support schemes for exporters.
• This surge is a positive indicator for India's economic recovery and its ambition to become a global manufacturing hub.
• However, concerns persist regarding global trade uncertainties and rising protectionism in some advanced economies.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.

articles.headline: India-Vietnam Bolster Trade and Strategic Ties Amid Indo-Pacific Focus
articles.explainer:

• Discussions focused on enhancing bilateral trade to $20 billion by 2030, with emphasis on pharmaceuticals, textiles, and agricultural products.
• Both nations reiterated their shared vision for a free, open, and rules-based Indo-Pacific, intensifying cooperation in maritime security.
• Agreements were signed on digital cooperation, renewable energy, and cultural exchanges, expanding the scope of their partnership.
• This deepening alliance is seen as crucial for regional stability and countering growing geopolitical complexities in Southeast Asia.

articles.headline: Government Unveils Draft National Framework for AI Ethics and Responsible Development
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) released a draft 'National Framework for AI Ethics and Responsible Development'.
• The framework proposes guidelines for ethical AI deployment across sectors, focusing on fairness, transparency, accountability, and data privacy.
• It aims to foster innovation while mitigating potential risks of AI, such as bias, discrimination, and job displacement.
• Public consultations are invited to ensure a holistic approach, involving industry, academia, civil society, and legal experts.
• This initiative positions India as a leader in developing responsible AI, aligning with its vision for 'AI for All'.

articles.headline: SEBI Proposes Enhanced Sustainability Reporting Framework for Listed Entities
articles.explainer:
• The Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) has released a consultation paper proposing significant enhancements to the Business Responsibility and Sustainability Reporting (BRSR) framework.
• The proposed changes aim to bring Indian companies in line with global sustainability reporting standards and improve transparency on ESG (Environmental, Social, and Governance) performance.
• Key proposals include mandatory reporting on Scope 3 emissions, climate-related financial disclosures, and social impact assessments.
• This move is expected to facilitate responsible investing, attract ESG-focused capital, and promote sustainable business practices.
• SEBI emphasizes the role of credible sustainability data in informed decision-making for investors and stakeholders.

articles.headline: India-Pakistan Hold Annual Talks on Indus Waters Treaty Amid Regional Challenges
articles.explainer:
• Delegations from India and Pakistan convened for their annual Permanent Indus Commission (PIC) meeting under the provisions of the Indus Waters Treaty.
• The talks focused on operational aspects of the treaty, proposed hydroelectric projects by both sides, and data exchange on river flows.
• Despite ongoing political tensions, the Indus Waters Treaty has largely endured as a testament to bilateral cooperation on shared resources.
• India reiterated its commitment to the treaty while emphasizing its right to utilize its share of waters for agricultural and power generation purposes.
• The meeting aimed to resolve minor technical disputes and ensure the smooth implementation of the landmark agreement.

articles.headline: Government Proposes Amendments to E-Waste Management Rules for Circular Economy
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change has proposed significant amendments to the E-Waste Management Rules, 2022.
• The amendments aim to strengthen the circular economy principles by promoting better collection, recycling, and refurbishment of electronic waste.
• Key changes include enhanced Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) targets, stricter penalties for non-compliance, and provisions for urban local bodies.
• The updated rules seek to address the rapidly increasing volume of e-waste and its associated environmental and health hazards.
• Stakeholder consultations are underway to finalize the amendments, ensuring a robust and effective regulatory framework.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the 'Bharat Logistics & Infrastructure Mission 2047', consider the following statements:
1. The mission aims to reduce India's logistics costs to 8% of GDP by 2047.
2. It integrates existing initiatives like PM Gati Shakti with new investments in multi-modal logistics.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct. The mission's key objective is to reduce logistics costs to 8% of GDP by 2047. Statement 2 is correct. It integrates PM Gati Shakti and includes new investments. Statement 3 is incorrect. The mission explicitly adopts a public-private partnership model, inviting significant private sector investment.

prelimsMocks.question: The 'Right to be Forgotten', recently clarified by the Supreme Court, is primarily related to which of the following?
options: (a) Right to silence during police interrogation
(b) Right to privacy in the digital age
(d) Right to anonymity in public protests
prelimsMocks.answer: (b) Right to privacy in the digital age
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Supreme Court's judgment on the 'Right to be Forgotten' specifically addresses an individual's right to have personal data deleted or de-indexed from public platforms in the digital realm, balancing it with the public's right to information. This falls under the broader ambit of the Right to Privacy (Article 21).

GS1.headline: Nation Remembers Tribal Freedom Fighter Birsa Munda on His Death Anniversary
GS1.explainer:
• Tributes were paid across the nation to legendary tribal freedom fighter Birsa Munda on his 125th death anniversary (June 9, 1900).
• Birsa Munda led the 'Ulgulan' (Great Tumult) movement against British colonial rule and the exploitation by local landlords and moneylenders in the late 19th century.
• He is revered as 'Dharti Abba' (Father of the Earth) by tribal communities, especially in Jharkhand, for his efforts to protect their land, culture, and rights.
• His movement sought to establish 'Munda Raj' and challenged the British legal system that alienated tribals from their traditional land and forest rights.
• The government highlighted his contributions to India's freedom struggle and reiterated commitment to tribal welfare and preservation of their heritage.

Region of activity: Chota Nagpur Plateau (present-day Jharkhand).
Fought against: British colonial rule, Dikus (outsiders).
mainsPoints: Discuss the nature and significance of tribal movements, such as the 'Ulgulan' led by Birsa Munda, in the context of India's freedom struggle.
Analyze how Birsa Munda's leadership addressed socio-economic and cultural exploitation faced by tribal communities under British rule.
Examine the enduring legacy of Birsa Munda and its relevance for tribal rights and welfare in contemporary India.
Critically evaluate the British policies that led to widespread tribal discontent and rebellions in the 19th century.

GS1.trivia: Birsa Munda is the only tribal leader whose portrait hangs in the Central Hall of the Indian Parliament.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Clarifies 'Right to be Forgotten' within Digital India Context
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court, in a landmark judgment, provided a detailed framework for the 'Right to be Forgotten' in India.
• The ruling emphasizes a balance between an individual's right to privacy and the public's right to information, especially for historical or public interest records.
• It clarifies that the right is not absolute and will be subject to conditions like the nature of information, its public relevance, and the impact on free speech.
• The court directed the government to consider legislative steps to incorporate this right explicitly within India's data protection framework.
• This judgment is expected to have significant implications for online content publishers, data aggregators, and social media platforms.

The right is not absolute and has conditions.

mainsPoints: Discuss the constitutional basis and jurisprudential evolution of the 'Right to be Forgotten' in India.
Analyze the challenges in balancing individual privacy rights with freedom of speech and public access to information in the digital age.
Examine the potential impact of this judgment on media, social media platforms, and the upcoming data protection legislation.
Suggest legislative and technological measures required for effective implementation of the 'Right to be Forgotten'.
GS2.imageDescription: A gavel resting on a legal book, symbolizing a Supreme Court judgment, with a blurred background of digital data.
GS2.trivia: The 'Right to be Forgotten' originated in European Union's General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR).

GS2.headline: Parliamentary Panel Recommends Reforms in Anti-Defection Law to Ensure Stability and Democracy
GS2.explainer:
• A Parliamentary Standing Committee on Law and Justice submitted a report recommending significant amendments to the Anti-Defection Law (Tenth Schedule).
• Key recommendations include restricting the Speaker's discretionary powers in defection cases and setting a strict time limit for decisions.
• The panel suggested that defection should only apply to votes on confidence motions or budget, not all legislative votes, to encourage intra-party dissent.
• It also proposed that independent committees or the Election Commission should adjudicate defection cases, rather than the Speaker.
• The proposed reforms aim to prevent political instability caused by frequent defections and uphold the spirit of parliamentary democracy.

GS2.headline: NEP 2020 Implementation: Focus on Foundational Literacy and Numeracy in Rural Areas
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education released a progress report on the implementation of National Education Policy (NEP) 2020, highlighting efforts in foundational literacy and numeracy (FLN).
• A special emphasis is being placed on rural and underserved areas to ensure equitable access to quality early childhood education.
• Initiatives include extensive teacher training programs, development of engaging learning materials in regional languages, and community participation.
• The 'NIPUN Bharat Mission' continues to be the cornerstone for achieving universal FLN by 2026-27.
• The report underlines the importance of integrating technology and play-based learning methods to make education more accessible and effective for young learners.

GS2.headline: India Deepens Energy Cooperation with Middle Eastern Nations Amid Global Volatility
GS2.explainer:
• India has intensified its energy diplomacy with key Middle Eastern countries to ensure stable and diversified energy supplies.
• Bilateral agreements focused on long-term oil and gas contracts, strategic petroleum reserves, and investments in renewable energy projects.
• The move is aimed at mitigating risks from global energy price volatility and geopolitical uncertainties.
• India emphasized its role as a reliable energy consumer and a growing market for green energy technologies.
• Discussions also included collaboration on petrochemicals, hydrogen, and carbon capture technologies, moving beyond traditional fossil fuels.

GS2.headline: High Court Directs States to Expedite Compensation for Victims of Mob Lynching
GS2.explainer:
• A prominent High Court issued directives to state governments to formulate and implement victim compensation schemes specifically for mob lynching incidents.
• The court expressed serious concern over the delay in providing financial aid and rehabilitation to victims and their families.
• It emphasized that compensation should be prompt, adequate, and not contingent upon the conviction of the accused, as per Supreme Court guidelines.
• The judgment highlighted the state's responsibility to protect its citizens and ensure social justice, especially for vulnerable sections.
• This ruling is expected to push states to adopt more proactive measures to address mob violence and support its victims.

GS2.headline: India-Vietnam Bolster Trade and Strategic Ties Amid Indo-Pacific Focus
GS2.explainer:
• India and Vietnam held high-level talks, reaffirming their commitment to strengthen comprehensive strategic partnership.
• Discussions focused on enhancing bilateral trade to $20 billion by 2030, with emphasis on pharmaceuticals, textiles, and agricultural products.
• Both nations reiterated their shared vision for a free, open, and rules-based Indo-Pacific, intensifying cooperation in maritime security.
• Agreements were signed on digital cooperation, renewable energy, and cultural exchanges, expanding the scope of their partnership.
• This deepening alliance is seen as crucial for regional stability and countering growing geopolitical complexities in Southeast Asia.

GS2.headline: India-Pakistan Hold Annual Talks on Indus Waters Treaty Amid Regional Challenges
GS2.explainer:
• Delegations from India and Pakistan convened for their annual Permanent Indus Commission (PIC) meeting under the provisions of the Indus Waters Treaty.
• The talks focused on operational aspects of the treaty, proposed hydroelectric projects by both sides, and data exchange on river flows.
• Despite ongoing political tensions, the Indus Waters Treaty has largely endured as a testament to bilateral cooperation on shared resources.
• India reiterated its commitment to the treaty while emphasizing its right to utilize its share of waters for agricultural and power generation purposes.
• The meeting aimed to resolve minor technical disputes and ensure the smooth implementation of the landmark agreement.

GS2.headline: India Pitches Digital Public Infrastructure Model for Global Adoption at UN Forum
GS2.explainer:
• India showcased its successful Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) model, including Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker, at a United Nations forum.
• The Indian delegation proposed the adoption of similar open, interoperable, and inclusive digital platforms by other developing nations.
• Emphasis was placed on how DPI can accelerate financial inclusion, efficient public service delivery, and foster innovation.
• India offered technical assistance and knowledge sharing to countries interested in replicating its DPI success stories.
• This initiative reinforces India's growing leadership in digital governance and its commitment to global digital transformation.

GS3.headline: PM Unveils 'Bharat Logistics & Infrastructure Mission 2047' to Boost Connectivity and Economy
GS3.explainer:
• Prime Minister launches ambitious 'Bharat Logistics & Infrastructure Mission 2047' aiming for world-class connectivity by India's centenary of independence.
• The mission integrates existing initiatives like PM Gati Shakti with new investments in multi-modal logistics parks, high-speed rail corridors, and port modernization.
• Focus on reducing logistics costs from 14% to 8% of GDP, enhancing global competitiveness and creating millions of jobs.
• Utilizes advanced technologies like AI, IoT, and blockchain for efficient supply chain management and project monitoring.
• Public-private partnership model to be adopted extensively, inviting significant private sector investment and technological expertise.

GS3.trivia: India's logistics sector is one of the largest employers, and its efficiency is crucial for the 'Make in India' initiative.

GS3.headline: National Green Hydrogen Mission Achieves Key Milestone, First Commercial Plant Operational
GS3.explainer:
• India's first large-scale commercial green hydrogen production plant, under the National Green Hydrogen Mission, has commenced operations in Gujarat.
• The plant utilizes renewable energy sources for electrolysis, producing green hydrogen with zero carbon emissions.
• This milestone is crucial for India's target of producing 5 million tonnes of green hydrogen annually by 2030.
• The project aims to decarbonize hard-to-abate sectors like refineries, fertilizers, and steel, while also fostering export opportunities.
• Government incentives and policy support have been instrumental in attracting private investment for such large-scale green energy projects.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Launches 'NISAR-2' Satellite for Advanced Earth Observation
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully launched 'NISAR-2', an advanced Earth observation satellite, from Satish Dhawan Space Centre.
• 'NISAR-2' is a follow-up to the joint ISRO-NASA NISAR mission, focusing on high-resolution imaging for climate studies and disaster management.
• The satellite is equipped with advanced Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) technology, enabling all-weather, day-night observations of Earth's surface.
• Data from NISAR-2 will be crucial for monitoring changes in ecosystems, ice-sheet collapses, natural hazards like earthquakes and tsunamis, and agricultural mapping.
• This mission reinforces India's capabilities in space technology and its commitment to global environmental monitoring.

GS3.trivia: NISAR stands for NASA-ISRO Synthetic Aperture Radar, a joint mission between the two space agencies.

GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate at 6.5%, Signals Vigilance on Inflationary Pressures
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) unanimously decided to keep the repo rate unchanged at 6.5%.
• This decision reflects the MPC's cautious approach amidst fluctuating global commodity prices and domestic food inflation concerns.
• RBI reiterated its commitment to aligning inflation with the target while supporting growth, maintaining its 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance.
• Experts suggest the central bank is closely monitoring monsoon progress and its impact on food prices, as well as global economic uncertainties.
• Future policy actions will be data-dependent, with a focus on ensuring financial stability and sustainable economic recovery.

GS3.trivia: The MPC has six members: three from RBI (including the Governor) and three external members appointed by the government.

GS3.headline: New Cyber Security Framework for Critical Infrastructure Rolled Out by CERT-In
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Computer Emergency Response Team (CERT-In) has released an updated and comprehensive cybersecurity framework for critical information infrastructure (CII).
• The framework mandates stringent security audits, real-time threat monitoring, and incident response protocols for sectors like power, finance, telecom, and transportation.
• It emphasizes a 'zero-trust' architecture and greater collaboration between government agencies and private sector operators of CII.
• The move comes amidst a rising number of sophisticated cyberattacks globally, posing significant risks to national security and economic stability.
• Training and capacity building for cybersecurity professionals within CII entities are also a key component of the new guidelines.

GS3.headline: India Pledges Support for Global Plastics Treaty at UNEP Negotiations
GS3.explainer:
• India reaffirmed its commitment to a legally binding global plastics treaty at the ongoing United Nations Environment Programme (UNEP) negotiations.
• The Indian delegation emphasized a comprehensive approach focusing on the entire lifecycle of plastics, from production to disposal.
• Key proposals from India include promoting circular economy principles, enhancing recycling infrastructure, and supporting developing nations with technology transfer and finance.
• India highlighted its domestic efforts, such as the ban on single-use plastics and extended producer responsibility (EPR) guidelines.
• The treaty aims to address plastic pollution, which poses a severe threat to marine ecosystems, human health, and climate.

GS3.headline: Indigenous mRNA Vaccine for Dengue Shows Promising Results in Phase-III Trials
GS3.explainer:
• India's first indigenous mRNA vaccine candidate for Dengue fever has demonstrated high efficacy and safety in its Phase-III clinical trials.
• Developed by a Pune-based biotechnology firm, the vaccine targets all four serotypes of the Dengue virus, offering broad protection.
• The success marks a significant leap in India's vaccine development capabilities, leveraging cutting-edge mRNA technology, previously used for COVID-19 vaccines.
• Regulatory approval is anticipated soon, paving the way for mass production and deployment to combat the perennial public health challenge of Dengue.
• This development is crucial for reducing the burden on healthcare systems and improving public health outcomes in Dengue-endemic regions.

GS3.headline: India's Exports Surge in May, Driven by Engineering Goods and Pharmaceuticals
GS3.explainer:
• India's merchandise exports witnessed a significant surge in May 2025, showing robust growth across several key sectors.
• Engineering goods, pharmaceuticals, and electronic items emerged as the primary drivers of this export growth, reflecting global demand.
• The Ministry of Commerce and Industry attributed the positive trend to diversified export markets and government support schemes for exporters.
• This surge is a positive indicator for India's economic recovery and its ambition to become a global manufacturing hub.

GS3.headline: Government Unveils Draft National Framework for AI Ethics and Responsible Development
GS3.explainer:

GS3.headline: SEBI Proposes Enhanced Sustainability Reporting Framework for Listed Entities
GS3.explainer:
• The Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) has released a consultation paper proposing significant enhancements to the Business Responsibility and Sustainability Reporting (BRSR) framework.
• The proposed changes aim to bring Indian companies in line with global sustainability reporting standards and improve transparency on ESG (Environmental, Social, and Governance) performance.
• Key proposals include mandatory reporting on Scope 3 emissions, climate-related financial disclosures, and social impact assessments.
• This move is expected to facilitate responsible investing, attract ESG-focused capital, and promote sustainable business practices.
• SEBI emphasizes the role of credible sustainability data in informed decision-making for investors and stakeholders.

GS3.headline: Government Proposes Amendments to E-Waste Management Rules for Circular Economy
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change has proposed significant amendments to the E-Waste Management Rules, 2022.
• The amendments aim to strengthen the circular economy principles by promoting better collection, recycling, and refurbishment of electronic waste.
• Key changes include enhanced Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) targets, stricter penalties for non-compliance, and provisions for urban local bodies.
• The updated rules seek to address the rapidly increasing volume of e-waste and its associated environmental and health hazards.
• Stakeholder consultations are underway to finalize the amendments, ensuring a robust and effective regulatory framework.
